content.problemStatement:
JJ gives Chef a number XXX and challenges Chef to come up with three **distinct** numbers A,A,A, B,B,B, and CCC such that:


0‚â§A,B,C<2300 \le A, B, C \lt 2^{30}0‚â§A,B,C<230;
(A‚äïB)+(B‚äïC)+(C‚äïA)=X(A \oplus B) + (B \oplus C) + (C \oplus A) = X(A‚äïB)+(B‚äïC)+(C‚äïA)=X.
Help Chef come up with three such numbers or determine that no such tuple exists.

Here, ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operation.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer XXX - the number mentioned in the problem statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output three **distinct** numbers AAA, BBB and CCC (0‚â§A,B,C<230)(0 \le A, B, C \lt 2^{30})(0‚â§A,B,C<230) such that (A‚äïB)+(B‚äïC)+(C‚äïA)=X(A \oplus B) + (B \oplus C) + (C \oplus A) = X(A‚äïB)+(B‚äïC)+(C‚äïA)=X.


If multiple such tuples exist, print any. If no such tuple exists, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: A=0,B=1,C=3A = 0, B = 1, C = 3A=0,B=1,C=3 is a valid answer because (0‚äï1)+(1‚äï3)+(3‚äï0)=1+2+3=6(0 \oplus 1) + (1 \oplus 3) + (3 \oplus 0) = 1 + 2 + 3 = 6(0‚äï1)+(1‚äï3)+(3‚äï0)=1+2+3=6.
Test Case 222: It can be proven that no tuple exists that satisfies the given conditions.
Test Case 333: A=3,B=11,C=1A = 3, B = 11, C = 1A=3,B=11,C=1 is a valid answer because (3‚äï11)+(11‚äï1)+(1‚äï3)=8+10+2=20(3 \oplus 11) + (11 \oplus 1) + (1 \oplus 3) = 8 + 10 + 2 = 20(3‚äï11)+(11‚äï1)+(1‚äï3)=8+10+2=20.
